title: Causal Attention
description: An attention pattern that lets each token read earlier tokens and itself, but not later tokens, so the model can predict text one step at a time.
openingSummary: Causal attention is the decoder-style attention pattern that blocks future tokens. Each position can use the tokens that already exist in the prefix and its own position, but it cannot peek at tokens that have not been generated yet.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Causal attention is the masked version of self-attention used for next-token prediction. For a query at position t, the mask keeps keys from positions 0 through t visible and blocks positions after t.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: The mask preserves the training and inference rule that the model must predict the next token from only the prefix it has already seen. Without that restriction, a decoder could cheat by reading the answer from later positions.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Queries, keys, and values are computed as in ordinary self-attention, but the score matrix is multiplied by a causal mask before softmax. That mask keeps the diagonal and everything to the left, while zeroing scores for future positions. The result is an attention pattern where each token can read its earlier context and itself, but not tokens that come later in the sequence.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: The formulas below contrast causal attention with bidirectional attention. The projections stay the same, but causal attention applies a triangular mask that blocks future positions, which is what makes token-by-token autoregressive generation possible.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Compared with bidirectional attention, causal attention gives up right-context access so the model can produce text one token at a time. Compared with the broader attention page, this page focuses on the no-looking-ahead mask rather than the full query-key-value mechanism.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: Causal attention appears in decoder-only transformer stacks and on the decoder side of encoder-decoder systems when the output must be generated step by step.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: Because each token cannot read future positions, causal attention gives up some contextual information that encoder-style full-context attention can use. Long outputs also require repeated decode steps, so latency depends on token-by-token generation rather than one full-sequence pass.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: Most chat and text-generation systems still rely on autoregressive decoding. Understanding causal attention explains why decoders need a key-value cache, why generation unfolds left to right, and why future tokens stay hidden during both training and inference.

computeFlow.alt: Causal attention versus bidirectional attention time reach
computeFlow.caption: Causal attention stops at the current token, while bidirectional attention can also read later positions.

causal.visibleContext: Earlier tokens plus the current token only
causal.bestFitTask: Decoder-style next-token prediction and autoregressive generation
causal.generationBehavior: Produces output one token at a time without looking ahead

bidirectional.visibleContext: Both earlier and later tokens in the same visible sequence
bidirectional.bestFitTask: Encoder-style understanding and masked language model objectives
bidirectional.generationBehavior: Not suitable for plain next-token decoding because future tokens stay visible

attention.visibleContext: Depends on the architecture and mask pattern chosen for the attention block
attention.bestFitTask: General attention lookup across many Transformer designs
attention.generationBehavior: Varies with the chosen mask, sequence source, and objective

gi.definition: Bidirectional mask that keeps both left and right context available inside the visible sequence.
